for_c_level: This run exposes high risk in testing infrastructure reliability, as zero scenario results were recovered despite requests. It blocks assessment of production readiness and undermines any customer-facing claims on AI memory sharing. Versus prior runs, this is a complete regression in harness execution.
for_non_technical: The test aimed to see if AI agents can reliably share memories with each other in a networked setup. Unfortunately, the test system broke down, and no results came back at all. We can't tell from this if the memory sharing works well or not.
for_sme: All 35 requested scenarios (S1, S1b, S2, S4-S6, S9-S18, S22-S25, S28-S42) failed to produce reports, impacting all primitives like federation mesh (W=2/N=4) on digitalocean nyc3. Probable root cause is artifact recovery failure in CI harness (sha: e8b9089a3c974746715d1ad0111a7d8be38f8c52); check workflow_url for logs. No specific failure modes observable due to total absence of data.
headline: Campaign failed: no scenario reports recovered.
next_run_change: Debug and fix the scenario report collection and recovery in the CI workflow before the next campaign.
verdict: FAIL — no scenarios executed or reported.
what_it_proved: The run demonstrated a critical failure in the campaign harness or reporting, proving nothing about AI memory functionality or reliability.
what_it_tested: This run requested 35 scenarios covering AI memory sharing across federation mesh topology but recovered no reports, exercising no transport, framework, or primitive axes.
